title: Custom Branding: Logo Upload, Dark Mode, and Primary Color Customization
story: As a product administrator, I want to customize the DataTools Pro branding with my organization's logo, favicon, primary color, and a dark/light mode toggle, so that the app aligns with my company's visual identity.
description:
Premium branding feature allowing team administrators to custom-brand their DataTools Pro instance. Available as a premium feature for paid customers.

## Features
1. **Custom Logo**: Upload a logo to replace the DataTools Pro header logo. Stored as base64 in the database (no file persistence on the app server). Supports delete to revert to default.
2. **Custom Favicon**: Upload a custom favicon. Also stored as base64. Supports delete.
3. **Dark Mode**: Toggle between light and dark layout modes. The existing theme partially supported dark mode, but required extensive CSS adjustments for custom styles.
4. **Primary Color**: Select a primary brand color from presets or enter a custom hex value. The selected color is applied globally to buttons, headings, clickable elements, and other branded UI components.

## Technical Approach
- Logos and favicons stored as base64 strings in the database to avoid file system dependencies and S3 setup
- Primary color implemented via a global CSS variable/class that replaces the default green throughout the app
- Dark mode required testing and fixing custom styles across all pages since the base theme's dark mode support did not cover custom overrides
- Branding settings page at `/brandings`
- Feature gated to paid customers via permission checks
acceptance_criteria: Branding settings page is accessible at /brandings
User can upload a custom logo that replaces the header logo site-wide
User can upload a custom favicon
Uploaded logos and favicons are stored as base64 in the database
User can delete custom logo/favicon to revert to defaults
User can toggle between dark and light mode
Dark mode applies correctly across all pages including custom-styled elements
User can select a primary color from presets
User can enter a custom hex value for primary color
Primary color applies globally to buttons, headings, and clickable elements
Branding feature is restricted to paid customers
Navbar renders correctly in dark mode
Object selection widget renders correctly in dark mode
ux_changes: New /brandings settings page with logo upload, favicon upload, dark/light mode toggle, and primary color picker (presets + custom hex). All branding changes apply globally across the application. Dark mode required extensive CSS adjustments for navbar and object selection widgets.
middleware_changes: Server-side handlers for logo/favicon upload (base64 encoding), branding settings persistence, and color theme application. Global CSS variable injection based on saved primary color.
data_model_changes: New branding settings stored in the database: logo (base64), favicon (base64), layout mode (dark/light), primary color (hex value).
api_changes: Branding settings endpoints: save/retrieve branding preferences, upload/delete logo, upload/delete favicon.
implementation_notes: Started as a spike to estimate effort for custom branding. Evolved into full implementation. Logos stored as base64 in the database to avoid S3 dependency — product owner suggested enforcing strict pixel size guidelines on import. Dark mode was partially supported by the base theme but required fixing many custom styles. Primary color change applied via a global CSS class/variable that overrides the default green. Feature gated behind paid subscription permissions.
comment_summary: Initially a spike to estimate effort. Product owner decided to proceed after initial assessment. Developer implemented dark mode, logo/favicon upload (base64 in DB), and primary color customization. Product owner noted dark mode was 95% complete with minor CSS tweaks needed on the navbar and object selection widget. Custom hex color input requested in addition to presets. Feature closed as successfully implemented.
